semanticCore: Weakness, frailty, splitting open, tearing apart, collapsing, or losing structural strength

keyDerivations.meaning: To become weak, frail, split, torn, or fall to pieces
Frail, weak, split open, or physically collapsing

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَانْشَقَّتِ السَّمَاءُ فَهِيَ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَاهِيَةٌ (69:16): And the sky will split open, and it on that Day is frail/weak.
summaryEn: The root represents structural collapse or extreme frailty (Wahy), describing how the heavens will split apart and lose their structural integrity on the Day of Resurrection.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الضعف والوهن والتشقق والتمزق وتداعي البنيان وسقوط القوة (وهي)، واصفاً تفتت السماء وتصدعها يوم القيامة بالواهية.
rawText: وَاهٍ Lax; weak: frail; wanting in strength, compactness, firmness, or toughness; unsubstantial; unsound. See an ex. in the S, voce أُنْشُوطَةٌ, where it is applied to love, or affection. === وَاهًا: see an ex. voce نُغْبَةٌ; and see آهِ, in art. اوه, in two places. وَهَى 1 It was, or became, much slit, or rent. (Msb.) === It was, or became, lax, flaccid, or flabby: (Msb:) or weak; said of a rope, (Mgh,) or other thing: or it fell. (Msb.) See وَاهٍ. === وَهَِىَ It was, or became, uncompact, unsound, or weak. === وَهَى أَمْرُهُ [His affair, or case, was, or became, in a weak, or an unsound, state]. (TA, art.نغض, &c.)
